positiveClaims: The existing release claim command admission gate is product-bound as bounded internal Review Bridge 12J command admission evidence.
Accepted 12J requires raw 12I kernel fence input and re-evaluates the 12I kernel fence internally before accepting command admission evidence.
Accepted 12J requires the internally evaluated 12I kernel fence result to have accepted kernel fence type, status, code, and reason.
Accepted 12J requires the internally evaluated 12I kernel fence binding fields for mode, claimId, dossierId, matrixId, and releaseClass.
Accepted 12J requires the internally evaluated 12I kernel fence summary fields for claimSurface, packetId, attestationId, and admissionClass.
Accepted 12J requires requestedMode to match the accepted 12I kernel fence mode.
Accepted 12J requires requestedClaimSurface to match the accepted 12I kernel fence claimSurface.
Accepted 12J requires commandId as command admission evidence identity only.
Optional kernelFenceResult input is consistency evidence only; stale, fabricated, inherited, or non-plain supplied values are blocked unless they match the internally re-evaluated 12I result.
PR_MODE USER_FACING command admission requests are blocked through the 12I kernel fence chain.
RELEASE_MODE USER_FACING command admission evidence is accepted only when the accepted 12I kernel fence releaseClass is USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY.
Accepted 12J emits a deterministic non-mutating command admission result with fixed top-level, binding, and summary keys.
The 12J command admission runtime now strips inherited prototype fields before command input and kernelFenceResult consistency checks.

This contour is release claim command admission evidence only; it is not command availability, command execution, product publication, release readiness, import, export, apply, storage truth, or project truth.
nonClaims: No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No release-wide publication authority is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim command admission binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim command admission binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim command admission binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Command admission binding is internal chain evidence only.
Command admission binding does not execute release commands.
Command admission binding does not admit release execution.
Command admission binding does not admit command availability.
Command admission binding does not publish anything.
Command admission binding does not become project truth by itself.
Command admission binding does not write manuscript truth or project truth.
12K release claim execution gate remains a separate downstream contour and is not product-bound by this contour.
USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY remains an internal packet, boundary, publication-gate, kernel-fence, and command-admission class only, not a release readiness state.
USER_FACING is a boundary, publication gate, kernel fence, and command admission surface in this contour, not a user-facing UI state.
This contour is not a release execution contour.
This contour is not a product publication contour.
This contour is not content import and does not create scenes.
This contour is not Review import automation and does not activate a Review session.
This contour is not an apply plan and does not authorize mutation.
This contour is not safe write and does not create receipt or recovery evidence.
This contour is not export and does not alter DOCX output.
Review Bridge may reference accepted 12J output as bounded internal chain evidence, but accepted 12J output does not become project truth.

machineChecks.command: node --test release claim command admission contracts
node --test downstream release claim execution gate contracts
node --test release claim 12I 12J 12K and command surface contracts
node --test release claim full contract pack

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed 12J accepted fully populated synthetic, stale, inherited, and class-instance kernelFenceResult envelopes because 12J trusted caller-supplied 12I result instead of raw upstream input. Recommended mirroring the 12I pattern for 12J and leaving 12K re-evaluation as a separate contour.

nextStep: select the next owner-approved boundary explicitly; 12K release execution binding, command execution, real product publication, and post-MVP import/export expansion do not open by implication
